text:
It has happened to me, but not often. I guess I use Slack enough to have developed reasonable habits. Maybe replies from recent members (if they are also recent Slack adopters) are more relevant here.

And then we should do some bikeshedding about the preferred content for the empty reply. Maybe an em-dash? Could it be just a non-breaking space?  :grin:

text: To belabour the point, here's a couple of screenshots that demonstrate the inconsistent UX I'm talking about. I used the word "button"  in the above, but that might be an incorrect term.  I click on the "NN replies" thingie (Perplexity calls it a "badge") in all cases except the first. In the first case, I see an empty text box immediately below the post and my instinct is to type into it and hit send. I think that I would favour seeing "0 replies" in the first case. Perplexity toes the party line saying that this "reduces visual clutter", and doesn't categorize this as bad UX design.
